scenarios.claim: The live gateway can authenticate the Telegram bot and deliver a diagnostic message to the configured operator target.
A real Telegram command can set the active operator session to the process runtime.
A real Telegram command can bind the process runtime to a provider-backed model route instead of the fail-closed local placeholder.
The selected provider-backed process model can complete a live chat request before the Telegram process prompt is queued.
A real Telegram operator prompt can queue a process-runtime chat run and receive a code-oriented answer.
A real Telegram operator command can create a Kanban/backlog task that is queryable through the gateway.
A real Telegram command can retrieve the current backlog snapshot after task creation.

redaction.trackedArchive: Tracked archives keep only pass/fail gates, redacted IDs, marker hashes, command outcomes, and item state. Telegram identifiers, raw prompts, message bodies, bot tokens, API tokens, and provider outputs are omitted.
